title: Log podman build failures in papr

comments.body: We finally have debug logs for FAH27!

@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat  We're getting a `time="2018-06-01T15:54:18Z" level=error msg="error reading from pipe 9: resource temporarily unavailable"` out of `podman build` - any idea what's going on?

That's odd. When I just redirect stderr to a file, I do get a backtrace in the file. Though that doesn't seem to be the case here. Anyway, you can see the actual backtrace I posted in #872.

Looking at #873, it looks like FAH28 isn't hitting this. Probably still worth investigating the failure, though.
Going to see if the failure is consistent or if we get different errors

The error you're seeing is happening when we're creating the container and are trying to capture the output from:

```
errorFds = []int{stdioPipe[unix.Stdout][0], stdioPipe[unix.Stderr][0]}
```
The code all looks to be OK, and not something that should fail.  The line it's failing on is:

```
nread, err := unix.Read(fd, buf)
```

and we've two instances so I'm not completely sure which of the two blew up..  I'm not sure about the why....
